contract.name: v2/agency/toptier_code/program_activity/count.md
contract.description: Returns the number of program activity categories for a specified agency toptier code in a given fiscal year.

toptier_code.description: Agency toptier identifier echoed in responses.
toptier_code.constraints: Required; accepts numeric digit strings of length 3 or 4 that map to an existing CGAC/FREC; codes with more than four digits are truncated to the last four before validation; malformed segments (non-numeric or missing leading zeros) fall through to an HTML 404 route.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year to evaluate; defaults to the API's latest reporting year when omitted or blank (observed 2026).
fiscal_year.constraints: Optional; accepts integers 2008 through 2026 inclusive as observed; duplicate query keys use the last value; unknown query parameters are ignored; non-integer values return HTTP 400; out-of-range integers return HTTP 422.

toptier_code.description: Echo of the requested toptier code.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year applied to the count.

program_activity_count.description: Number of distinct program activity categories for the agency and fiscal year.

messages.description: Informational or warning strings; pre-2017 fiscal years emit the documented DATA Act warning.

messages: Account data powering this endpoint were first collected in FY2017 Q2 under the DATA Act; as such, there are no data available for prior fiscal years.

quirks: Blank fiscal_year query values are treated as omitted and default to the latest reporting year.
When fiscal_year is provided multiple times, the last value wins.
Unknown query parameters are silently ignored.
Requests for fiscal years before DATA Act reporting emit a standard warning message in messages.
Malformed toptier path segments (non-numeric or missing zero padding) return an HTML 404 page instead of JSON.
Supplying more than four digits in toptier_code causes the value to be truncated to the last four digits before validation, so the 404 detail references the truncated code.
risks: Clients expecting JSON must guard against HTML 404 responses when the path segment fails routing.
Fiscal year bounds track the platform's active reporting year and may shift, leading to 422 responses for stale integrations.
A valid response can legitimately report a zero count, so downstream logic must distinguish zero data from errors.
Implicit truncation of overlong toptier codes can obscure the true source of 404 errors during debugging.
gaps: No evidence on alternate warning messages beyond the pre-2017 DATA Act notice.

probes.notes: Omitted fiscal_year defaulted to FY 2026 with zero count.
Explicit fiscal_year within range returns populated count.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"toptier_code":"012","fiscal_year":2015,"program_activity_count":0,"messages":["Account data powering this endpoint were first collected in FY2017 Q2 under the DATA Act; as such, there are no data available for prior fiscal years."]}

probes.notes: Pre-2017 fiscal years return zero counts plus DATA Act warning.
Valid 4-digit toptier defaults to the latest reporting year.
4-digit toptier with explicit fiscal_year returns data.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Agency with a toptier code of '999' does not exist"}

probes.notes: Unknown numeric code returns JSON 404 detail.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'fiscal_year'. 'abc' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: Missing leading zeros causes routing miss and HTML 404.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Agency with a toptier code of '0000' does not exist"}

probes.notes: Overlong digit string truncated to last four digits before lookup.

mismatches: Docs present a JSON request schema of type number, but the live GET endpoint is driven solely by path and query parameters.
Docs describe toptier_code as a number, yet the API expects zero-padded digit strings and returns the value as a string.
gaps: No evidence on alternate warning messages beyond the pre-2017 DATA Act notice.
risks: Clients expecting JSON must guard against HTML 404 responses when the path segment fails routing.
Fiscal year bounds track the platform's active reporting year and may shift, leading to 422 responses for stale integrations.
A valid response can legitimately report a zero count, so downstream logic must distinguish zero data from errors.
Implicit truncation of overlong toptier codes can obscure the true source of 404 errors during debugging.
